results.title: Is Topline legit?
results.content: - I agree. While I don't think it's a scam, the pay-rate for Topline seems excessively low. I'll grant that it's completely passive, so the argument could be made that it's money coming in that wouldn't normally be there. But at the same time, like most reddit users, I'm on the interwebzz a lot, and all that browsing data/adspace has got to be worth more than the 0.03/day I'm getting, no? Replacing all of my ads every time I browse is only worth ~$10 after six months of use everyday? Really? At the end of the day, it seems like taking the risk of opening myself up to having possibly non-vetted ads for that kind of money might not be worth it. I turk and use some of the other sites like Treasuretrooper and Naturebucks and Hitsforpay and Bing Rewards with a lot more success. So it's clear [...] - It's not a scam but it's incredibly hard to make $10 to cash out. In my experience, the performance hit from the flash ads wasn't worth the money I've made. I have 48 referrals on topline, and I've had the account for about the same time as my Naturebucks nonref account. Even though my naturebucks account has far less referrals (30), I've made much more. With topline, I have made about $1.04 from referrals. From Naturebucks, I've made about $39.00 from referrals. And quite honestly, I put a lot more effort into my topline account than my naturebucks account, to my disappointment. For those interested, here is a non-referral link for naturebucks. Here is my referral link [...] and Bing Rewards with a lot more success. So it's clear that I'm willing to do menial, but easy, tasks for pennies at a time. But these other sites (naturebucks in particular) have given me a hell of a lot more back in referral bonuses. With their current structure, if I'm earning 5% per level 1 referral, I stand to earn, what, 50 cents per 6-month time period? That seems paltry, and bordering on insulting, which is why I don't try to gain referrals for Topline. I saw the AMA from the Topline guy and the business seems to be reputable- the fact that he was willing to address the beermoney subreddit was a good sign. I still have Topline installed and think their model is great, but in the end, if the payouts aren't more reasonable, I think I might jump ship after I hit the $10 mark (circa

results.title: Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic ⇒ Pley.gg
results.content:
### Pley Admin

Pley Admin is the official publishing profile for Pley.gg, used for articles, updates, and general site communications. Content published follows our editorial standards and is maintained for clarity and consistency across our site.

SPORTZINO Banner
Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

Pley is your all-in-one hub for competitive shooters. Get the latest news, tournament coverage, expert guides, player profiles, and betting insights across Counter-Strike 2, Valorant, and Apex Legends. Whether you’re following the pro scene or looking to improve your own gameplay, Pley delivers accurate updates, tactical tips, and exclusive content to keep you informed and ahead. [...] Despite the pro-players wishes to remove the map from the professional map-pool, statistics show that the map was played the most over the past 12 months at big events between the top 20 teams. It was played a total of 16 times with Nuke and Inferno settling at second place, having been played 11 times. This shows just how frustrating it must be for some teams to play on Dust 2, but also how immortal and an integrated part of the Counter-Strike scene, the map has grown to become.

Watch and learn more about Dust 2’s history as well as its best and worst moments here:

Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic
Dust 2: Frustrating, Broken and Iconic

### Pley Admin [...] However, with the release of CS:GO the game of Counter-Strike became more and more competitive and thereby also studied and analyzed more. The more strategic approach seemed to be the general ideal approach for the professional teams and players, which unfortunately meant little praise of the iconic map Dust 2, which enabled more individual skill-based play with its open spaces.

Many professional players have stated that they would like to see Dust 2 removed, in order to further embrace the more strategic approaches, that new and different maps bring to the table.
